rationale: The citing paper uses Berger and Milkman (2012) mainly as an example that content characteristics (e.g., valence) can affect sharing, to contrast prior "what is shared" work with its own network-overlap focus. This is a background positioning use in the literature review/introduction, not a citation the paper builds on for hypotheses, mechanisms, measures, or modeling choices. The cited paper is not engaged in the empirical analysis or interpretation of results, so the role is primarily a passing contextual mention.
